title: Hybrid adoption without a rewrite
summary: A brownfield system still has legacy internals, but explicit contracts and runtime enforcement now govern the boundaries where change matters.

services.summary: Legacy implementation kept in place behind a governed contract boundary.
Governed façade that isolates legacy behavior and exposes one explicit contract line.
New capability introduced without rewriting the legacy core.

runtime_decisions: Allowed the legacy core to remain because the governed façade defines the external behavior explicitly.
Validated all consumer interactions at the boundary rather than rewriting the old implementation first.
Recorded hybrid adoption evidence so operators can see which parts are governed and which remain legacy.
expectations: Hybrid adoption is acceptable when contracts and runtime enforcement govern the interaction boundaries.
Chapter 11 does not require a rewrite; it requires changing how the system evolves.
The reader should leave this lab understanding that coherence can expand incrementally across a brownfield system.
